title: invAIriant Audit — conveyor (pre-GA hardening)
scope: Scheduler control plane, worker runtime, tenant API, and artifact distribution at a41f9cc, against README/ARCHITECTURE/THREAT_MODEL. Out of scope: billing exporter, Terraform, web console.
participants: claude-agent (lens auditor: parnas, turing, von-neumann, lamport, review-bottleneck)

lens_scores.verdict: THREAT_MODEL row 'credential exposure via logs' closed on paper only; refuted by worker/recover.go:88-104 (CNV-041)
boundaries hold; one leak in api/handlers/jobs.go:210-232 (CNV-044, S2)
changes localized, ADRs current; two CHANGELOG omissions (obs O-2)
dispatch/retry loops bounded; scheduler-decision replay tooling missing (obs O-1)
Redis cache treated as truth on recovery (CNV-043)
webhook retry lacks end-to-end idempotency key (CNV-042)

findings.claim: The worker crash-recovery path serializes the entire job environment, including tenant-scoped API keys, into the shared operator log.

evidence.description: log.Errorw("job crashed", "env", job.Env) where job.Env carries CONVEYOR_TENANT_KEY
THREAT_MODEL marks 'credential exposure via logs' mitigated by env scrubbing that does not exist

findings.risk: Any operator and the log-pipeline vendor can read live tenant credentials; INC-118 already wrote 7 tenants' keys to retained logs.
findings.recommendation: Scrub/allowlist env at the logging boundary; rotate exposed keys; add a no-secrets conformance test; fix THREAT_MODEL only after the control exists.

verification.method: read worker/recover.go and worker/exec.go; grepped for scrub; cross-read THREAT_MODEL:141; confirmed INC-118 excerpt

findings.claim: Webhook delivery retries re-POST without an idempotency key, so a timeout-then-success sequence applies tenant side effects twice.

evidence.description: retry loop re-sends the identical request; no Idempotency-Key header
no test under distrib/ exercises timeout-after-delivery

findings.risk: Tenants with non-idempotent webhook actions get duplicate side effects during network degradation.
findings.recommendation: Add a per-delivery idempotency key, document the contract, add the timeout-after-delivery test.

findings.claim: On restart the scheduler rebuilds its run queue from the Redis cache instead of Postgres, so cancellations that raced the crash are resurrected.

evidence.description: queue rebuilt exclusively from cache.ScanJobs(); Postgres reconciliation is a TODO at line 95
ARCHITECTURE says Postgres is the single source of truth for job state and Redis is a disposable accelerator; recovery reads Redis as truth

findings.risk: Restart changes the meaning of the system: cancelled jobs run, violating tenant expectations and — for destructive jobs — tenant data.
findings.recommendation: Rebuild the queue from Postgres, then warm the cache; add a crash-cancel conformance test; implement the TODO at scheduler/recover.go:95.

verification.method: read scheduler/recover.go:61-97; cross-read ARCHITECTURE.md:203; ran the cancel-then-SIGKILL repro (3/3)

findings.claim: Tenant API handlers construct scheduler-internal types directly, bypassing the submission contract.

evidence.description: imports scheduler/internal/model and sets private queue hints

findings.risk: Scheduler refactors now break the API layer, and queue hints become an undocumented tenant-visible behavior.
findings.recommendation: Extend the public submission DTO with the one hint actually needed; add an import-boundary lint rule (depguard).

verification.method: read api/handlers/jobs.go:210-232; confirmed the scheduler/internal import

observations.text: Scheduler decisions are not replayable: inputs logged but the decision-function version is not.

hypotheses.text: Dispatch loop starves low-priority tenants under saturation
hypotheses.rejected_reason: fairness property test dispatch/fairness_test.go:12-88 exists and passes at a41f9cc

hypotheses.text: semgrep: possible SSRF in artifact fetch (distrib/fetch.go:40)
hypotheses.rejected_reason: URL host pinned to tenant-registered endpoints validated at registration (api/tenants.go:118-141); scanner unaware of the invariant

summary.executive_summary: Module boundaries and construction discipline are solid for pre-GA, but the audit fails the gate on CNV-041 (tenant API keys written to the shared operator log on worker crash). Two S1s compound it: double-firing webhooks (CNV-042) and Redis-as-truth on recovery (CNV-043). A high average lens score does not offset the S0.
summary.weakest_reason: a threat-model row was closed on paper while the control did not exist (CNV-041) — exactly what this lens exists to catch

required_actions.action: Scrub env at log boundary + rotate exposed keys + add log-schema conformance test
Recovery rebuilds from Postgres, then warms the cache; add a crash-cancel conformance test
Public submission DTO + depguard import-boundary rule
